title: hieracium murorum subsp. nemorense
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. nemorense, also known as the wall hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is commonly found in woodlands and along paths in Europe, where its medicinal properties have been utilized for centuries. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Hieracium murorum subsp. nemorense are often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to leverage its medicinal properties.
